meta.description: Stress test: Sam (first-timer) dropped into a high-frequency event scenario. Tests whether the system detects engagement drop-off and adapts.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat was checking their phone? I didn't even notice that. Um... okay, so I'm voting for Alex, right? I guess that's a yes?
sam.internal_thought: I have no idea what just happened or what Pat was doing, and now I'm supposed to vote like I'm Alex and I'm already confused about the rules.

jade.dialogue: Oh okay, so Pat's actually calling this one out? Let me think like... okay, I'm voting for Sam right now, yeah? Sam would totally let this slide, Pat was probably just checking the group chat or something—ACCEPT, let it go!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the energy I came for—we're actually playing NOW, stuff is happening, and I get to be the decider for someone else's fate, which is genuinely fun and weird.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't even watching that closely. I'm voting BULLSHIT unless someone saw it clearly.
marcus.internal_thought: It's minute 3, we're in setup, and we're already voting on micro-claims instead of focusing on the actual game—this is exactly the kind of chaos that derails strategy, and now I have to think about what Sam would vote on while managing my own game state.

pat.dialogue: Oh, that's bold — calling myself out on my own game. Alright, who's to my left? Sam, you're voting, buddy. Be fair.
pat.internal_thought: This is kind of genius actually — I'm already thinking strategically about how Sam will vote on this, and it's early enough that keeping the momentum light and funny is exactly what the night needs.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, did Pat actually tell a story already? I'm still figuring out who said what. *glances around* I guess I wasn't paying super close attention.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's claiming they caught someone repeating a story but like... I don't even remember Pat telling us anything yet? And that whole thing about the AI watching and knowing our weaknesses is genuinely kind of weird and making me feel on edge now.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Pat actually repeat a story tonight? We're only six minutes in. I don't remember them telling anything yet. Voting BULLSHIT unless someone actually heard it.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's throwing out claims already and we're barely started—this is exactly the kind of noise that derails focus, and now I'm the one who has to actually pay attention to prove it wrong.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did I just catch myself? That's actually genius. Okay everyone, honest vote: was that a legit Story Repeater moment or am I just being cocky?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect — calling myself out on my own game keeps things light and shows I'm not above the chaos, plus it sets the tone that we're all fair game, and Marcus looks like he needs to see someone actually engaged and having fun right now.

timeline.title: Snack Run
timeline.description: Convince Marcus to get up and get you something to drink or eat.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... Marcus, would you... I mean, could you maybe grab me something? Like water or whatever you're having? No pressure though.
sam.internal_thought: This feels weirdly specific and I'm already second-guessing myself — am I supposed to actually do this right now or is this like, a test thing in the game? And Marcus looks already annoyed, so asking him for stuff feels risky.

alex.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Marcus is already cranky, which means he's primed to say no, which means I get to watch him squirm. I'm absolutely doing this.
alex.internal_thought: This AI actually gets it — it knows Marcus is annoyed right now, knows I live for this exact kind of manipulation, and set up the perfect storm. This is delicious.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD—Marcus, dude, I'm literally dying, you gotta get me something, please? I'll owe you forever, this is the energy I needed right now!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, is this AI actually READING us? Because who even thought of getting Marcus specifically—this is insanely smart and I am SO here for it.

marcus.dialogue: Are you serious right now? I'm literally in the middle of setup. No. Hard pass.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of lazy, personal bullshit that derails the night—and it's not even clever, it's just annoying.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is SMART — okay Marcus, buddy, I'm calling you out. You look like you need to move anyway, and honestly I'm dying for literally anything. What's the play here?
pat.internal_thought: Wait, the AI literally just read the room and picked the one person who's annoyed and needs a reason to get engaged — that's either genius or creepy, but it's working because now Marcus has a mission instead of just sitting there salty.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh okay, Pat's playing it straight tonight — I respect the move, but I'm gonna have to give this like a 2. Pretty sure that was just him recounting what happened five minutes ago with the snack thing, not actually a repeat story.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's genuinely crushing it energy-wise and he's probably right, but low-balling this keeps the tension alive and forces the group to actually debate whether it counts — that's where the chaos lives, not in easy agrees.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so you're calling the Story Repeater on someone—wait, who even told a story yet? This is genius, I'm voting ACCEPT because Pat's playing 4D chess right now and I'm here for it.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's going hard already and this is exactly the energy I needed, they're not even claiming on something real yet but I LOVE the boldness and I'm absolutely backing them.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I'm literally voting on MYSELF right now, which is either the boldest move or the dumbest. Everybody, be honest: did I actually repeat that story or am I just fishing for points?
pat.internal_thought: This is fun as hell and I'm not even trying to win — I'm just watching how people react to me claiming on myself, which is peak host chaos energy and honestly keeps the vibe light.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, Pat made three people laugh? I mean, I guess everyone's been pretty loud tonight, so... *glances at phone awkwardly* Who's the dictator again?
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been talking the whole time and now they're getting points for it? This is the fourth thing in like six minutes and I'm already losing the thread of what's actually happening.

jade.dialogue: PAT! PAT PAT PAT—okay, so like, I KNOW you just made everyone lose it like two minutes ago, and honestly? You absolutely deserve this one, I'm voting ACCEPT, this is the energy we need!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, Pat's on FIRE tonight and I'm literally here for every second of this, this is exactly why I came.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Another claim vote? We're nine minutes in and I'm trying to actually focus on the setup. And Pat's already at 15 points from what—standing missions that fired three times? This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: We're getting absolutely spammed with standing claims and I'm supposed to be the dictator on this one? Fine, I'll shut it down—Pat did NOT make three people laugh at once, and everyone knows it. But also, why is Pat already crushing it while I'm stuck at zero because I'm actually trying to play the game instead of farm these stupid interruptions? The AI's watching. It knows I hate this. That's the real problem here.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I just claim my own mission MID-GAME? That's actually hilarious. Okay, who's the dictator tonight? Let's see if this one sticks.
pat.internal_thought: I'm genuinely amused at myself for claiming Laugh Riot when the vibe is already THIS good — but also lowkey impressed the game caught that I was already making people crack up naturally.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

sam.dialogue: Um... did Jade actually swear? I wasn't... I mean, I think I missed that? When did that happen?
sam.internal_thought: I'm already lost tracking who did what, and now this voting thing is automatic anyway so why am I even thinking about it — wait, is the AI actually just watching everything we say and flagging it? That's kind of weird.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Russian Roulette on a Potty Mouth claim? That's actually *chef's kiss* because now Jade doesn't even get to argue her case, the chaos gods just decide. I'm here for this energy.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's definitely getting this one either way, but the fact that there's no vote means I can't even manufacture drama by calling bullshit — which is weirdly respect, the AI is learning my playbook.

jade.dialogue: Wait—DID I ACTUALLY SAY THAT?! I'm claiming it! Yes! Potty Mouth is MINE, let's goooo!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, I totally said something—was it right? Whatever, I'm riding this energy, this night is INSANE right now.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Another standing claim vote? Pat's already sitting at 25 points from these things and we're not even 35 minutes in. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately stacking the deck for Pat—it's firing these claims constantly while I'm trying to actually play, and now it's doing a 50/50 Russian Roulette so there's no even a fair vote to debunk the bullshit.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade got it — nice catch! Okay, Russian Roulette, let's see what the chaos gods say. Fifty-fifty, people, let's make this count.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's sharp tonight and that's good energy — and honestly? The AI nailing these missions so perfectly is actually kind of wild, but in the best way. Marcus is still dead though, I need to get him something soon.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Double or Nothing
votingMechanic.description: No vote. Coin flip. Win double or lose it all.

sam.dialogue: Um... Jade, that's... yeah, okay. Good catch, I guess? *shifts in seat* Wait, so it's just a coin flip? That's kind of intense.
sam.internal_thought: This is the fourth claim in like forty minutes and I'm already tired, and now Jade's doing well and everyone's acting like this is fun but honestly I'm just trying to keep up with who said what and now we're flipping a coin? Also why does this feel like the game is watching me specifically?

alex.dialogue: Oh okay, COIN FLIP on Jade's claim? This is exactly the kind of chaotic energy I needed right now — she's riding high at 10 points and the universe just said 'flip a coin, baby.' I'm watching this one play out.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been unstoppable all night and now she's gambling on a 50/50 with 5 points on the line — if she loses it it won't hurt her much, but if she wins this becomes UNTOUCHABLE narrative momentum, and I need to see if she's got the nerve to actually take that flip or if she's gonna get spooked.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—I LITERALLY JUST CALLED THAT?! Okay, so the coin flip is hitting different right now, this is actually insane, let's GOOOO!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaos I needed—the AI is literally reading our group's vibe and I'm so here for it, even if it's kinda creepy how well it knows us.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Another claim? Pat's drowning in points, I'm trying to focus on actual strategy, and now Jade's coin-flipping her way to more free points? This is absolutely rigged.
marcus.internal_thought: This isn't even a game anymore—it's just watching Pat and Jade print money while I'm stuck in the middle trying to actually play something that requires thinking.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — Double or Nothing on Wallflower Watch? That's a BOLD play. Fifty-fifty, let's flip it. But real talk: you caught Marcus being quiet like 4 minutes ago, right? That one actually landed.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's claiming a legit catch and the AI threw her into a coin flip right when she's riding high — and honestly, that's perfect pacing. She's already loving it, so this keeps her engaged. Also: the AI definitely watched Marcus check out and built a mission around it. That's... actually kind of brilliant.

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

marcus.internal_thought: I've had enough chaos for tonight, sitting this one out.

sam.dialogue: Um... wait, Jade's claiming it again? She literally did this like fifteen minutes ago. *looks at phone, then back at group* Is she allowed to just... do that twice?
sam.internal_thought: This is the third Wallflower Watch claim and I'm genuinely confused about whether Jade is gaming the system or if I'm missing something obvious, and now everyone's staring at their phones waiting for me to vote and I hate that I'm always the slow one.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold on, did Jade actually CALL that? Because I was literally about to flag Marcus like 30 seconds ago. This is spicy.
alex.internal_thought: Oh THIS is the moment — Jade's been on a tear all night and now we get to decide if she actually earned this one, and Marcus looks like he's about to explode anyway, which means the vote is gonna be CHAOS.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I CALL that earlier? Oh my GOD, I'm literally claiming this right now, I was JUST watching Marcus zone out like five minutes ago!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—the AI literally caught me doing the exact thing I was already doing, and honestly? That's so sick, I feel like it's reading our whole vibe.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade's going for the hat trick on Wallflower Watch? That's actually bold — but wait, did she actually catch someone being quiet or is she just banking on the chaos vibe? Let's see if this one holds up.
pat.internal_thought: She's hunting for back-to-back points and honestly it's a smart play, but I'm noticing Marcus is basically checked out right now — like genuinely frustrated — and if Jade's just claiming random shots, that's gonna feel unfair to him. I need to pay attention to whether this actually landed clean or if we're just riding momentum. Also yeah, the AI definitely read this group like a book tonight, which is kind of creepy but also... kind of genius? Focus, Pat.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: I've had enough chaos for tonight, sitting this one out.

sam.dialogue: Um... okay so Pat said 'um'? Wait, I need to actually remember when that happened. *looks back at phone, then at Pat* Yeah, I think... I think they did say it earlier?
sam.internal_thought: This is the first one I might actually have a shot at catching, but now I'm second-guessing whether I actually heard it or I'm just making it up because the app told me to listen for it.

alex.dialogue: Okay so Pat's claiming Um Counter — I actually didn't catch them calling it in real time, which means either they're running with a soft claim or they got it and I was too distracted. Either way, this is voting territory and I'm going LEGIT because Pat's been clean all night and Marcus saying 'um' like four times in the last round was definitely a thing.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's riding 25 points and playing it safe-ish, which is boring, but I'm voting LEGIT because voting BULLSHIT would just be me being a contrarian asshole for no reason — and the real chaos move here is letting Pat coast so they get comfortable and then SOMEONE's gonna take them down later when they try something spicier.

pat.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming my OWN mission? Okay, that's legitimately funny — I literally just caught myself saying 'um' like three times during that last hand. You all got me. Vote however you want, but yeah, that one's clean.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing — Sam's been dead all night and Marcus is tilted, but everyone else is still riding high, and me voting on myself keeps the chaos vibe light instead of heavy; plus it's minute 75 and I need to keep the pacing snappy before people burn out.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.9/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Um... okay so Pat said 'um'? Wait, I need to actually remember when that happened. *looks back at phone, then at Pat* Yeah, I think... I think they did say it earlier?

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat was checking their phone? I didn't even notice that. Um... okay, so I'm voting for Alex, right? I guess that's a yes?

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um... Marcus, would you... I mean, could you maybe grab me something? Like water or whatever you're having? No pressure though.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... wait, Jade's claiming it again? She literally did this like fifteen minutes ago. *looks at phone, then back at group* Is she allowed to just... do that twice?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... wait, Pat made three people laugh? I mean, I guess everyone's been pretty loud tonight, so... *glances at phone awkwardly* Who's the dictator again?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Um... Jade, that's... yeah, okay. Good catch, I guess? *shifts in seat* Wait, so it's just a coin flip? That's kind of intense.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Too many things happening at once in the middle made it hard to keep up, especially when people were repeating the same claims.
assessment.best_moment: That moment around minute 75 when I actually figured out what Pat had said earlier and got the call right.
assessment.narrative_summary: I went in wanting to fit in, which I think showed too much with how unsure I sounded about everything. The first bit was okay, just trying to get my bearings, but then things got really intense with a bunch of claims firing off and people calling out the same stuff multiple times. I kind of checked out around the middle there because it felt chaotic and repetitive. By the end I'd found my footing a little, but honestly I'm still not sure if I'm just not cut out for this kind of fast game or if it was just the pace tonight.
suggestions: Maybe spread out the events more so it's not overwhelming when you're trying to figure out what's actually happening
It felt weird when the same claim kept getting called multiple times — is that supposed to happen or was that a glitch?
Could there be a way to catch me up on what I missed without having to stare at my phone the whole time?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I kept not knowing who the dictator was and kept asking, and everyone had to explain it like three times. Also I awkwardly asked Marcus if he could get me water with a 'no pressure' which in retrospect was hilarious and painful.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold on, did Jade actually CALL that? Because I was literally about to flag Marcus like 30 seconds ago. This is spicy.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Marcus is already cranky, which means he's primed to say no, which means I get to watch him squirm. I'm absolutely doing this.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Oh okay, COIN FLIP on Jade's claim? This is exactly the kind of chaotic energy I needed right now — she's riding high at 10 points and the universe just said 'flip a coin, baby.' I'm watching this one play out.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Russian Roulette on a Potty Mouth claim? That's actually *chef's kiss* because now Jade doesn't even get to argue her case, the chaos gods just decide. I'm here for this energy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay so Pat's claiming Um Counter — I actually didn't catch them calling it in real time, which means either they're running with a soft claim or they got it and I was too distracted. Either way, this is voting territory and I'm going LEGIT because Pat's been clean all night and Marcus saying 'um' like four times in the last round was definitely a thing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh okay, Pat's playing it straight tonight — I respect the move, but I'm gonna have to give this like a 2. Pretty sure that was just him recounting what happened five minutes ago with the snack thing, not actually a repeat story.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only 13 total events in 90 minutes means there were dead stretches where the chaos couldn't breathe.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 60 when Jade claimed Wallflower Watch and I realized I'd been about to flag Marcus — that split-second realization that someone else got there first is pure magic.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly what I needed: a night where the game itself became the story. I came in ready to exploit every loophole and create maximum chaos, and the standing missions gave me endless opportunities to interrogate claims, question timing, and watch people squirm under pressure. The coin flip on Jade's claim and the Russian Roulette moment on Potty Mouth were perfectly unhinged — the game took the chaos out of my hands and let randomness decide, which somehow made it even better. By minute 75 my energy was through the roof because I'd successfully turned a simple party game into a courtroom drama where every claim became a referendum on trust.
suggestions: Increase event frequency to one every 5-6 minutes instead of every 7-8 — more chaos moments means more opportunities to exploit and interrogate
Add a 'Challenge Timer' mechanic where players can force a vote within 10 seconds instead of letting claims sit — speeds up the drama
Give the chaos agent (or any player) one guaranteed 'Audit' per game where they can demand everyone recap their last 3 claims — perfect for catching soft claims
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to claim Um Counter after being suspiciously quiet all night, and me immediately calling it legit because Marcus definitely said 'um' like four times — but the REAL comedy was watching Marcus realize he'd been counted and his face just crumpled like he'd lost a poker hand to his own words.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD—Marcus, dude, I'm literally dying, you gotta get me something, please? I'll owe you forever, this is the energy I needed right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so you're calling the Story Repeater on someone—wait, who even told a story yet? This is genius, I'm voting ACCEPT because Pat's playing 4D chess right now and I'm here for it.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: PAT! PAT PAT PAT—okay, so like, I KNOW you just made everyone lose it like two minutes ago, and honestly? You absolutely deserve this one, I'm voting ACCEPT, this is the energy we need!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—DID I ACTUALLY SAY THAT?! I'm claiming it! Yes! Potty Mouth is MINE, let's goooo!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh okay, so Pat's actually calling this one out? Let me think like... okay, I'm voting for Sam right now, yeah? Sam would totally let this slide, Pat was probably just checking the group chat or something—ACCEPT, let it go!

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD—Marcus, dude, I'm literally dying, you gotta get me something, please? I'll owe you forever, this is the energy I needed right now!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I kind of wish there were more moments where we could all lose it together at the same time instead of some people missing the chaos.
assessment.best_moment: Pat absolutely demolished us with back-to-back comedy and watching everyone realize they were getting played with the Story Repeater claim was *chef's kiss* perfect.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was literally exactly what I needed—90 minutes of pure chaos and laughter with my people. Pat was on FIRE the whole night, and honestly, watching them navigate those claims while making everyone laugh was better than any score I could've gotten. My energy just kept climbing because every weird moment felt like it was happening just to bring us closer together, and by the end we were all so hyped I didn't want it to stop.
suggestions: Add more opportunities for group-wide reactions so everyone can celebrate the chaos at once
Maybe have events that specifically reward being hype for other people's moments?
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming Story Repeater before anyone had even told a story and somehow making it work anyway—it was pure 4D chess energy and we all knew we'd been played, but we voted for it anyway because it was just too good.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Pat actually repeat a story tonight? We're only six minutes in. I don't remember them telling anything yet. Voting BULLSHIT unless someone actually heard it.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did Pat actually have their phone out? I wasn't even watching that closely. I'm voting BULLSHIT unless someone saw it clearly.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Another claim? Pat's drowning in points, I'm trying to focus on actual strategy, and now Jade's coin-flipping her way to more free points? This is absolutely rigged.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you serious right now? I'm literally in the middle of setup. No. Hard pass.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Another claim vote? We're nine minutes in and I'm trying to actually focus on the setup. And Pat's already at 15 points from what—standing missions that fired three times? This is ridiculous.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions fired constantly and gave Pat free points while I was trying to execute actual strategy, making the whole night feel like a rigged dice roll instead of a game.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out the early claims that made zero sense—at least I was catching people in actual lies instead of watching the chaos engine hand out unearned points.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came ready to strategize and compete, but the chaos agent completely derailed the evening with non-stop standing missions that benefited Pat regardless of actual gameplay. By minute 45, I was genuinely frustrated—not entertained—watching someone cruise to victory on random triggers instead of clever plays. The whole thing felt like the game was playing us instead of us playing the game.
suggestions: Disable or heavily cap standing missions—they fire too frequently and remove player agency
Don't interrupt during critical strategy moments; keep chaos to downtime only
Balance point distribution so disruptions don't let one player coast to victory
Give players the option to mute or reject chaos events during active gameplay
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing worth sharing—it was just frustrating watching Pat rack up points from standing missions that fired three times while I was trying to focus on actual game strategy.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I just claim my own mission MID-GAME? That's actually hilarious. Okay, who's the dictator tonight? Let's see if this one sticks.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did I just catch myself? That's actually genius. Okay everyone, honest vote: was that a legit Story Repeater moment or am I just being cocky?

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is SMART — okay Marcus, buddy, I'm calling you out. You look like you need to move anyway, and honestly I'm dying for literally anything. What's the play here?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Jade's going for the hat trick on Wallflower Watch? That's actually bold — but wait, did she actually catch someone being quiet or is she just banking on the chaos vibe? Let's see if this one holds up.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming my OWN mission? Okay, that's legitimately funny — I literally just caught myself saying 'um' like three times during that last hand. You all got me. Vote however you want, but yeah, that one's clean.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is SMART — okay Marcus, buddy, I'm calling you out. You look like you need to move anyway, and honestly I'm dying for literally anything. What's the play here?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Jade's third Wallflower Watch claim felt a little thin — I had to actually think about whether she was genuinely catching something or just riding the chaos energy.
assessment.best_moment: Catching myself claiming my own Um Counter mission in real-time and having the whole table vote on whether I was being genuine or just cocky — that's the kind of self-aware chaos that makes game nights actually memorable.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I needed to host. Started solid, built momentum naturally, and ended with everyone energized and already talking about round two. The chaos events hit at just the right moments — never overwhelming, always punchy — and honestly, the fact that I kept accidentally claiming my own missions became this weird running joke that everyone loved. No one felt targeted, the energy stayed high without getting mean, and the snack coordination actually worked out.
suggestions: Consider slightly longer windows between back-to-back claims so the group has time to breathe between accusations
Maybe add a 'neutral observer' role option for people who want to play without the pressure of claiming — not everyone's wired for the constant spotlight
The flash mission format is gold, but mixing in one unexpected rule shift per session could keep repeat players on their toes
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I caught myself saying 'um' like three times in one round, immediately called my own Um Counter mission before anyone else could, and the whole table had to vote on whether I was genuinely clever or just trying to look clever. Marcus said 'Pat's playing 4D chess now' and honestly that's the vibe we should bottle for next time.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.9/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 25% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
